```py pyscripts/shoppingcart-project-report.py
from __future__ import annotations

import csv
from typing import Any, TypeAlias

from django.db.models import Prefetch, QuerySet
from django.utils import timezone

from cloud.contracts.models.contract import Contract
from cloud.planning.models.project import Project
from cloud.shoppingcart.interface import get_cart_totals

RowType: TypeAlias = Project


def fmt(value: Any) -> str | int | None:
    from datetime import date, datetime
    from decimal import Decimal
    from enum import Enum
    from uuid import UUID

    from django.db import models

    from choicesenum import ChoicesEnum
    from djmoney.money import Money

    if value is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(value, datetime):
        return value.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%f")
    if isinstance(value, date):
        return value.isoformat()
    if isinstance(value, (UUID, models.Model, Decimal)):
        return str(value)
    if isinstance(value, Money):
        return str(value.amount)
    if isinstance(value, (ChoicesEnum, Enum)):
        return value.value
    return value


def show_discounts(discount):
    if discount.amount is not None:
        return (
            discount.name,
            discount.product_category.display,
            f"{discount.amount.amount}",
        )
    if discount.num_terms_affected is not None:
        return (
            discount.name,
            discount.product_category.display,
            discount.num_terms_affected,
        )
    if discount.multiplier is not None:
        return (
            discount.name,
            discount.product_category.display,
            f"{discount.percent}%",
        )
    return None


def send_csv_to(email: str, csv_data: str):
    from django.core.mail import EmailMessage

    today = timezone.now().date()
    email_message = EmailMessage(
        f"Shoppingcart report - {today}",
        "All shoppingcart projects",
        email,
        [email],
        None,
    )
    email_message.communication_handler = None  # type: ignore
    email_message.attach(f"shoppingcart - {today}.csv", csv_data, "text/csv")
    email_message.send()


def get_csv():
    print(timezone.now())
    from io import StringIO

    from click import progressbar

    buf = StringIO()
    out = csv.DictWriter(buf, fieldnames=get_header())
    out.writeheader()

    data = get_data()

    with progressbar(data, length=data.count()) as bar:
        for element in bar:
            out.writerow({k: fmt(v) for k, v in make_row(element).items()})
    print(timezone.now())
    return buf


def get_header() -> list[str]:
    return [
        "project_number",
        "created_at",
        "invoiced_at",
        "business_unit",
        "country",
        "purchase_model",
        "subsidy",
        "workstation",
        # "sales_agent",
        # "primary_agent",
        "cloud_url",
        "total_gross_price",
        "total_gross_price_ex_vat",
        "total_cost",
        "total_installer_cost",
        "currency",
        "has_pv",
        "has_battery",
        "has_ev_charger",
        "system_size (Wp)",
        "extra_items list of (category, cost, price)",
        "discounts (name, category, amount)",
    ]


def make_row(p: RowType) -> dict[str, Any]:
    cart = p.current_shopping_cart
    assert cart
    spec = cart.old_spec
    contract = p.contract
    assert contract, p.id
    gross_prices = get_cart_totals(shopping_cart=cart, purchase_model=p.purchase_model)
    items = list(
        cart.extra_items.values(
            "product_category", "category", "unit_cost", "unit_price"
        )
    )
    items = [
        (
            i["product_category"].display,
            i["category"].display,
            str(i["unit_cost"]),
            str(i["unit_price"]),
        )
        for i in items
    ]

    mods = [
        show_discounts(i)
        for i in cart.get_applicable_discounts(main_purchase_model=p.purchase_model)
    ]
    mods = [m for m in mods if m]

    return {
        "project_number": p.project_number,
        "created_at": p.created_at,
        "invoiced_at": p.invoiced_at,
        "business_unit": p.business_unit.slug,
        "country": p.business_unit.market,
        "purchase_model": contract.purchase_model,
        "subsidy": contract.subsidy,
        "workstation": p.station_or_abandoned_value,
        "sales_agent": p.contract.interest.agent,
        "primary_agent": p.agent,
        "cloud_url": p.get_absolute_url(),
        "total_gross_price": gross_prices["total_gross_price"].amount,
        "total_gross_price_ex_vat": gross_prices["total_gross_price_excl_vat"].amount,
        "total_cost": gross_prices["total_cost"].amount,
        "total_installer_cost": gross_prices["total_installer_cost"].amount,
        "currency": gross_prices["total_cost"].currency,
        "has_pv": spec.has_pv_system,
        "has_battery": spec.has_batteries,
        "has_ev_charger": spec.has_ev_chargers,
        "system_size (Wp)": spec.get_system_power(),
        "extra_items list of (category, cost, price)": items,
        "discounts (name, category, amount)": mods,
    }


def get_data() -> QuerySet[RowType]:
    return (
        Project.objects.filter(current_shopping_cart__isnull=False)
        .select_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__business_unit",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__installer",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__cost_model",
            "business_unit",
        )
        .prefetch_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__extra_items",
            "current_shopping_cart__cart_modifications",
            Prefetch(
                "contract_set",
                queryset=Contract.project_objects.select_related(
                    "subsidy",
                    "interest__agent"
                ),
                to_attr="active_contracts",
            ),
        )
    )


buf = get_csv()
send_csv_to("rix1@otovo.com", buf.getvalue())
```

I recently added 'sales_gent' and 'primary_agent' to this report. Should I do any other changes to my `prefech_related` or `select_related` to avoid n+1 queries? I want this to be speedy af.
To optimize your database queries and avoid the N+1 query problem, you should ensure you are using `select_related` for related objects whenever you're accessing foreign key relationships directly in your code and `prefetch_related` for many-to-many or one-to-many relationships. 

In your case, since you added `sales_agent` and `primary_agent`, it seems like they might be related to your `Contract` or `Project` models. Let's optimize accordingly. Here's how you can enhance your existing code:

### Check the Project and Contract Models
Before modifying the queries, you should know the exact field names and relationships between `Project`, `Contract`, and the fields `sales_agent` and `primary_agent`. Assuming these fields exist in the `Contract` and `Project` models, you might need to update your query like this:

### Update in `get_data()`:

```python
def get_data() -> QuerySet[RowType]:
    return (
        Project.objects.filter(current_shopping_cart__isnull=False)
        .select_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__business_unit",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__installer",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__cost_model",
            "business_unit",
            "agent",  # Assuming this is the 'primary_agent' field in your Project model
        ).prefetch_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__extra_items",
            "current_shopping_cart__cart_modifications",
            Prefetch(
                "contract_set",
                queryset=Contract.objects.select_related(
                    "subsidy",
                    "interest__agent",  # Assuming this is the 'sales_agent' in the Contract model
                ),
                to_attr="active_contracts",
            ),
        )
    )
```

### Code Refactoring:
Here’s the modified snippet where the `get_data()` method is updated for optimized fetching:


Make sure that `sales_agent` and `primary_agent` indeed exist in the respective models and are relationships that can be optimized using `select_related`.

### Verification and Adjustment:
Based on your original snippet and these changes:
- If `sales_agent` is part of the `Contract` model and accessible through `interest__agent`.
- If `primary_agent` is directly on the `Project` model as `agent`.

You can modify accordingly if they are structured differently. Check the Django model fields to ensure that your `select_related` or `prefetch_related` are applied appropriately based on the relationships.

### Conclusion:
By adding these fields to `select_related` and `prefetch_related`, you reduce unnecessary database hits, turning potential N+1 queries into a single query, thus boosting performance.
so interest is actually a FK on the contract model, and the interest model in turn have interest.agent which is a customer.
Given that `interest` is a foreign key on the `Contract` model and `interest.agent` represents the customer or the `sales_agent`, we need to ensure that the query uses `select_related` to fetch the `interest` and further extends into `interest.agent`.

To effectively handle this, update your code to include these relationships in the `select_related` clause, which ensures that these related fields are fetched in the same query, preventing the N+1 problem.

Here's how you can adapt your `get_data()` function considering the relationships you've described:

### Update in `get_data()` Function:

```python
def get_data() -> QuerySet[RowType]:
    return (
        Project.objects.filter(current_shopping_cart__isnull=False)
        .select_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__business_unit",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__installer",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__cost_model",
            "business_unit",
            "agent",  # Assuming this is the 'primary_agent' field in your Project model
        )
        .prefetch_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__extra_items",
            "current_shopping_cart__cart_modifications",
            Prefetch(
                "contract_set",
                queryset=Contract.objects.select_related(
                    "subsidy",
                    "interest",  # Prefetch interest to use it for accessing 'agent'
                    "interest__agent"  # Prefetch interest's agent
                ),
                to_attr="active_contracts",
            ),
        )
    )
```

### Details:
- `select_related("interest", "interest__agent")`: Ensures that the `interest` and its related `agent` are fetched in a single database query.
- `agent`: Assuming this field is the `primary_agent` in the `Project` model.

### Full Updated Code Snippet:
Here’s the complete `get_data()` function within your report generation script:

```python
def get_data() -> QuerySet[RowType]:
    return (
        Project.objects.filter(current_shopping_cart__isnull=False)
        .select_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__business_unit",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__installer",
            "current_shopping_cart__cost__cost_model",
            "business_unit",
            "agent",  # Assuming this is the 'primary_agent' field in your Project model
        )
        .prefetch_related(
            "current_shopping_cart__extra_items",
            "current_shopping_cart__cart_modifications",
            Prefetch(
                "contract_set",
                queryset=Contract.objects.select_related(
                    "subsidy",
                    "interest",  # Ensure fetching of 'interest'
                    "interest__agent"  # Ensure fetching of 'agent' through 'interest'
                ),
                to_attr="active_contracts",
            ),
        )
    )
```

### Verifying the Make Row Function:
Additionally, ensure that you access the agents correctly in the `make_row` function:

```python
def make_row(p: RowType) -> dict[str, Any]:
    cart = p.current_shopping_cart
    assert cart
    spec = cart.old_spec
    contract = p.contract
    assert contract, p.id
    gross_prices = get_cart_totals(shopping_cart=cart, purchase_model=p.purchase_model)
    items = list(
        cart.extra_items.values(
            "product_category", "category", "unit_cost", "unit_price"
        )
    )
    items = [
        (
            i["product_category"].display,
            i["category"].display,
            str(i["unit_cost"]),
            str(i["unit_price"]),
        )
        for i in items
    ]

    mods = [
        show_discounts(i)
        for i in cart.get_applicable_discounts(main_purchase_model=p.purchase_model)
    ]
    mods = [m for m in mods if m]

    return {
        "project_number": p.project_number,
        "created_at": p.created_at,
        "invoiced_at": p.invoiced_at,
        "business_unit": p.business_unit.slug,
        "country": p.business_unit.market,
        "purchase_model": contract.purchase_model,
        "subsidy": contract.subsidy,
        "workstation": p.station_or_abandoned_value,
        "sales_agent": contract.interest.agent,  # Accessing sales_agent through interest
        "primary_agent": p.agent,  # Direct access to primary_agent
        "cloud_url": p.get_absolute_url(),
        "total_gross_price": gross_prices["total_gross_price"].amount,
        "total_gross_price_ex_vat": gross_prices["total_gross_price_excl_vat"].amount,
        "total_cost": gross_prices["total_cost"].amount,
        "total_installer_cost": gross_prices["total_installer_cost"].amount,
        "currency": gross_prices["total_cost"].currency,
        "has_pv": spec.has_pv_system,
        "has_battery": spec.has_batteries,
        "has_ev_charger": spec.has_ev_chargers,
        "system_size (Wp)": spec.get_system_power(),
        "extra_items list of (category, cost, price)": items,
        "discounts (name, category, amount)": mods,
    }
```

### Conclusion:
By improving the queryset with `select_related` and `prefetch_related` for these relationships, you will be reducing the number of database hits significantly and thus enhancing the performance of your report generation.
